info.name: Wagr USSD (simulated Moolre callbacks)
info.description:
Simulated Moolre USSD callbacks against the local Wagr api. While we don't have a paid Moolre USSD short code + ngrok, this collection replaces the real carrier callback so we can exercise /ussd end-to-end. Import this file via Postman → Import → File.

## Variables
- `api_url` — base URL of the running Wagr api. Default `http://localhost:3001`.
- `session_id` — Moolre session id. Reuse the same value across a flow to simulate the same dial.
- `msisdn` — Moolre MSISDN of the dialling worker (12 digits, 233...). Set this to a value that maps to an employee in your dev DB.

## Flow shape (after [ussd-balance-step])
- Worker with a PIN already set: new session → balance screen → press 1 → amount step (stubbed).
- Worker on first use: new session → PIN setup (enter, confirm) → balance screen → press 1.
- Worker with max_advance = 0: new session → END 'no advance available'.
- Unregistered msisdn: new session → END 'not registered'.

See docs/architecture/moolre-api-reference.md for the canonical callback shape.

item.name: USSD — new session (registered worker, PIN set)

request.description: First hit. If the employee exists, is active, and has a PIN set, expect the balance screen with name, earned wage, max advance, and 'Press 1 to continue.' (`reply: true`).
Press 1 on the balance screen. Returns the amount-step stub with `reply: false` (becomes the real amount step when [ussd-amount-step] lands).
Any input other than 1. Re-shows the balance screen with `reply: true` (session stays open).

item.name: USSD — PIN setup, enter new PIN

request.description: Step 1 of first-use PIN setup. Run after a new session against an employee with ussd_pin_hash=null. Expect 'Re-enter your PIN to confirm:' with `reply: true`.
Confirm step with a different PIN than was entered. Expect 'PINs did not match.' and a re-prompt back to step 1, `reply: true`.

item.name: USSD — PIN setup, confirm match (chains to balance)

request.description: Confirm step with the same PIN entered in step 1. The PIN is hashed and saved as a side effect, and the worker is dropped straight into the balance screen with `reply: true`. (If max_advance is 0 the session ends here with 'no advance available' instead.)
Sanity check on the Zod validator. Expect 400 with `{ error: { code: 'INVALID_BODY', message: 'msisdn: Required' } }`.
